title: Strawberry Shortcake Parfaits
imagePrompt: Four clear glass parfaits show distinct layers: golden-brown shortcake crumbs at the bottom, pale ivory vanilla cream in the middle, and deep red macerated strawberries with glossy pink syrup pooling between the layers. A final dollop of cream crowns each glass, dusted with more crumbs and topped with a single glistening strawberry slice.
subtitle: Macerated citrus strawberries, whipped vanilla bean cream, and buttery baked shortcake crumbs layered in clear glasses.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Bake the crumbs first — macerate strawberries and whip cream while they cool. Assemble right before serving so the crumbs stay crisp.
items: Vanilla bean cream
1 cup heavy whipping cream, chilled
½ cup crème fraîche (or full-fat Greek yogurt)
3 tbsp powdered sugar
1 tsp vanilla bean paste (or pure vanilla extract)
Zest of 1 organic lemon, finely minced
Macerated citrus strawberries
1 lb fresh strawberries, hulled and sliced
3 tbsp granulated vanilla sugar (or regular sugar)
1 tbsp fresh lemon juice
Golden shortcake crumbs
1½ cups all-purpose flour
3 tbsp granulated sugar
1½ tsp baking powder
¼ tsp fine sea salt
6 tbsp cold unsalted butter, cut into small cubes
⅓ cup ice-cold buttermilk
ingredients.callout: Crunch shortcut: Substitute baked crumbs with crumbled premium shortbread or graham crackers for a faster, ultra-crisp layer.

body.html: A gourmet take on strawberry shortcake built as layered parfaits: buttery baked crumb clusters, macerated strawberries in glossy citrus syrup, and lemon-kissed vanilla bean cream whipped to medium-stiff peaks.

items: Bake shortcake crumbs until deeply golden and crisp
Macerate strawberries with sugar and lemon juice
Whip vanilla bean cream with crème fraîche and lemon zest
Layer twice in clear glasses and serve immediately

body.html: Timing trick: Start the crumbs in the oven, then macerate berries and whip cream while they bake and cool.

slides.title: Bake the Shortcake Crumbs
slides.checkboxLabel: Bake shaggy dough crumbs until golden

items: Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment.
Whisk flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt in a medium bowl.
Pinch cold butter into the flour until it resembles coarse, pea-sized sand.
Make a well, pour in buttermilk, and stir with a fork just until a shaggy dough forms.
Crumble dough by hand onto the sheet in irregular, bite-sized clumps.
Bake 15–18 minutes until deeply golden brown and crisp.
Cool completely on a wire rack.

body.html: Don't overwork the dough. Shaggy and irregular = better crumb texture. You want distinct clusters, not a smooth ball.

slides.title: Macerate the Strawberries
slides.checkboxLabel: Toss berries with sugar and lemon, rest 15–20 min

items: Combine strawberries, sugar, and lemon juice in a glass bowl.
Toss gently to coat evenly.
Cover and rest at room temperature 15–20 minutes.
Sugar draws out juice — you want a bright, glossy strawberry syrup.

body.html: Herbal variation: Slap a few basil or mint leaves to release their oils, then toss into the berries while they macerate for a clean, sophisticated back-note.

slides.title: Whip the Vanilla Bean Cream
slides.checkboxLabel: Whip to medium-stiff peaks, chill until assembly

items: In a chilled glass or metal bowl, combine cream, crème fraîche, powdered sugar, vanilla, and lemon zest.
Whip with a hand mixer on medium speed 3–4 minutes until medium-stiff peaks form.
Peaks should hold their shape elegantly — not loose, not overwhipped and grainy.
Refrigerate until ready to assemble.

body.html: Crème fraîche adds tang and stability. Greek yogurt works in a pinch — the cream will be slightly sharper but still delicious.

slides.title: Assemble the Parfaits
slides.checkboxLabel: Layer crumbs, cream, berries — repeat and garnish

items: Use four clear 8-ounce glasses or jars to show the layers.
First layer: Generous spoonful of cooled shortcake crumbs.
Second layer: Thick layer of vanilla bean cream.
Third layer: Macerated strawberries with a drizzle of glossy syrup.
Repeat all three layers to fill the glasses.
Finish with a dollop of cream, a sprinkle of crumbs, and a glossy strawberry slice on top.
Serve immediately while crumbs are crisp.

body.html: Include the syrup. Spoon it over each berry layer so it seeps down — that's what ties the parfait together.
